**Wilson Gutiérrez** (born 5 May 1971) is a Colombian [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") manager[[1]](#cite_note-1) and former player.

He is best known for coaching [Independiente Santa Fe](/wiki/Independiente_Santa_Fe "Independiente Santa Fe"), and with this team, winning the [Apertura segment](/wiki/2012_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A_season#Finals "2012 Categoría Primera A season") of the [2012 Categoría Primera A](/wiki/2012_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A_season "2012 Categoría Primera A season"). It was the team's seventh title, but the first ince e1975. The club then qualified for the [2013 Copa Libertadores](/wiki/2013_Copa_Libertadores "2013 Copa Libertadores").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

He spent his active career in [CD Condór](/w/index.php?title=CD_Cond%C3%B3r&action=edit&redlink=1 "CD Condór (page does not exist)") [[es](https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Club_Deportivo_El_C%C3%B3ndor "es:Club Deportivo El Cóndor")], Santa Fe, [Atlético Huila](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Huila "Atlético Huila") and [La Equidad](/wiki/La_Equidad "La Equidad") until retiring in 2005.[[4]](#cite_note-career-4)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Wilson_Guti%C3%A9rrez&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Personal life")]

He grew up in the Pontevedra neighborhood of Bogotá.[[4]](#cite_note-career-4) He is nicknamed "El Flaco", albeit mostly to his closest friends. After leaving [Alianza Petrolera](/wiki/Alianza_F.C._(Colombia) "Alianza F.C. (Colombia)") in 2021, he moved to [Miami, Florida](/wiki/Miami,_Florida "Miami, Florida") to be close to his children, who already lived there.[[5]](#cite_note-5)
